---
title: ""
subtitle: ""
page-layout: full
toc: false
---

::: {.grid}
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4 .text-center}
![](headshot.jpg){#img-me .rounded-circle .img-fluid width="180px"}

### Shreyas Meher

**Postdoctoral Researcher**
Erasmus University Rotterdam

[meher@essb.eur.nl](mailto:meher@essb.eur.nl)
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-8}
### About

I am a Postdoctoral Researcher in the [Policy, Politics, and Society](https://www.eur.nl/en/essb/about-essb/academic-teams/policy-politics-and-society) group at Erasmus University Rotterdam. I earned my Ph.D. in Public Policy & Political Economy from the University of Texas at Dallas in 2025.

My research applies computational methods to substantive questions in digital governance, political conflict, and international political economy. It has appeared in *Political Analysis*, *Research & Politics*, and *Behavioral Sciences*. My foundational work on NLP for political science was developed through the NSF-funded [ConfliBERT project](https://eventdata.utdallas.edu/) with [Patrick T. Brandt](https://www.utdallas.edu/~pbrandt/).

At Erasmus, I work on the [TWIN4DEM project](https://twin4dem.eu/) with [Clara Egger](https://claramarieegger.wordpress.com/) and [Asya Zhelyazkova](https://asyazhelyazkovasite.wordpress.com/), building machine learning frameworks for modeling democratic processes and assessing institutional resilience.

[Digital Governance]{.interest-pill}
[Computational Social Science]{.interest-pill}
[NLP & LLMs]{.interest-pill}
[Democratic Backsliding]{.interest-pill}
[Democratic Backsliding]{.interest-pill}
[Reinforcement Learning]{.interest-pill}

:::
:::
